concepts: range-failure reversal after multi-bar breakout loses acceptance and re-enters balance
trend reset continuation after a deeper pullback instead of shallow ema-touch logic
compression fakeout into re-expansion with directional confirmation
management_styles: one-shot entry with partial take-profit and runner
scale-in on improved confirmation, then scale-out into expansion
partial-profit first target with runner managed by structure instead of fixed take-profit only

next_cycle_focus: Keep only mechanisms that prove non-sparse participation on their best-fit lane. If deep-reset continuation is still too thin on TAO, rotate away from continuation and lean harder into reacceptance and failed-auction families. If ETH range-failure and AXS fakeout re-expansion both trade credibly, refine management before altering base trigger grammar.
rationale: Cycle 66 is an explore cycle inside a strict AXS/ETH/TAO 4h basket with explicit concept targets: range-failure reversal, deeper pullback reset continuation, and compression fakeout re-expansion. The live authority files also explicitly warn against sparse decorative logic and permit best-fit lane choice across the basket. This batch therefore assigns each mechanism to the lane where it is most plausible: ETH 4h for failed-auction reacceptance in pure chop, TAO 4h for deeper reset continuation because it is the only lane with live trend-up contamination, and AXS 4h for fakeout-reexpansion because it is the transition-heavy structurally different lane. The three specs are materially distinct by mechanism, indicator role assignment, and exit structure while remaining strictly inside the supported/fetchable universe.
